description: Cross-family off-target NOMINATION benchmark over FOUR unbiased genome-wide assays. Task: rank a guide's candidate sites so the experimentally validated off-targets surface first. Labels = validated-active off-targets from published assays (NON-circular: the label is the wet-lab assay call, not a predictor).
label: active (1 = experimentally validated off-target, 0 = candidate not detected)
split: held-out-guide (a guide's candidates never straddle train/test)
leakage_control: CRISOT-Score is the MD-physics, assay-AGNOSTIC scorer (not fit on these labels); GUIDE/CIRCLE-seq use canonical guides, CHANGE/SITE-seq use INDEPENDENT broad guide panels -> cross-assay generalization. A genomic-coordinate locus split is not available (the harmonized data ships sequences, not coordinates); held-out-guide + cross-assay is the implemented leakage-clean evaluation.
baseline: sequence-homology nomination (ascending mismatch count over the 20-nt protospacer)
learned: CRISOT-Score (Chen et al., Nat Commun 2023, 10.1038/s41467-023-42695-4; MD-physics RNA-DNA fingerprint; CC-BY-NC, run on the VM, only derived scores cached)
metric: per-guide AUPRC; held-out-guide bootstrap (1000) CI on mean(CRISOT - homology) AUPRC excludes 0 on ALL FOUR assays
honesty: nomination is NOT a clearance; fixture actives/inactives are downsampled for a CI-safe committed file, the authoritative numbers are the full-data metrics computed on the VM.
